meaning: n.v.使劲投掷 <英>劣等廉价葡萄酒
examples: She sat down with a plonk.
Give the i-Plonk a nice waterproof cover.
True, it will lift the guilt off your shoulders, but it will plonk a whole heap of pain on your partner’s.
It is similar to the cheap plonk sold in France and other European winemaking countries.
Around noon, henna artists find a moment of calm to plonk down stools and draw intricate temporary tattoos freehand.
